title: Alfred Editor Walking Skeleton - M1: Rust Kernel
description: Implement the Rust kernel for the Alfred editor: Cargo workspace, rope-based buffer, cursor movement, viewport scrolling, terminal rendering, and event loop. M1 proves the system can display a file and navigate it.

steps.title: Cargo workspace and crate scaffolding
steps.description: Create the Cargo workspace with 5 crates: alfred-core (pure domain), alfred-lisp (stub), alfred-plugin (stub), alfred-tui (terminal shell), alfred-bin (entry point). alfred-core depends on ropey. alfred-tui depends on alfred-core, crossterm, ratatui. alfred-bin depends on all crates. Stub crates have lib.rs with a placeholder pub function.
acceptance_criteria: Cargo workspace structure: 5 crates in crates/ (alfred-core, alfred-lisp, alfred-plugin, alfred-tui, alfred-bin)
alfred-core Cargo.toml specifies ropey as sole external dependency
alfred-tui Cargo.toml specifies alfred-core, crossterm, and ratatui dependencies
alfred-core has zero runtime dependencies on alfred-tui, alfred-lisp, or alfred-plugin (verified by build)
alfred-bin main() entry point exists and successfully links all crates

steps.title: Buffer type wrapping ropey Rope
steps.description: Implement the Buffer type in alfred-core. Buffer wraps ropey::Rope with metadata (id, filename, modified, version). Provide pure functions: Buffer::from_file(path) -> Result<Buffer>, buffer::get_line(buffer, n) -> Option<&str>, buffer::line_count(buffer) -> usize, buffer::content(buffer) -> String. Buffer is immutable-friendly: operations return new Buffer instances where practical.
acceptance_criteria: Buffer::from_file loads a text file into a Rope
buffer::get_line returns the correct line content
buffer::line_count returns accurate count
Buffer stores filename and reports modified=false after loading
Loading a nonexistent file returns an error, not a panic

steps.title: Cursor and Position types with movement logic
steps.description: Implement Cursor (line, column) and movement functions in alfred-core. All functions are pure: cursor::move_up(cursor, buffer) -> Cursor, cursor::move_down(cursor, buffer) -> Cursor, cursor::move_left(cursor, buffer) -> Cursor, cursor::move_right(cursor, buffer) -> Cursor, cursor::ensure_within_bounds(cursor, buffer) -> Cursor. Movement clamps to buffer boundaries (no negative, no past-last-line, no past-line-length).
acceptance_criteria: move_down from line 0 returns Cursor at line 1
move_up from line 0 returns Cursor still at line 0 (clamped)
move_right from end-of-line: if next line exists, move to (line+1, 0); if at last line, clamp to current line end
move_left from start-of-line: if previous line exists, move to (line-1, end); if at first line, clamp to column 0
ensure_within_bounds clamps cursor when buffer shrinks

steps.title: Viewport with scrolling logic
steps.description: Implement Viewport (top_line, height, width, gutter_width) in alfred-core. Pure function: viewport::adjust(viewport, cursor) -> Viewport that ensures the cursor is always visible. If cursor moves above top_line, scroll up. If cursor moves below top_line + height, scroll down. Viewport is a simple value type.
acceptance_criteria: Viewport with top_line=0, height=24: cursor at line 25 causes top_line to adjust
Cursor at line 0 with top_line=10: top_line adjusts to 0
Cursor within viewport: no viewport change
Viewport adjustment is pure (returns new Viewport, does not mutate)
Viewport gutter_width initialized to 0 in M1 (reserved for M4 line-numbers plugin)

steps.title: KeyEvent, CommandRegistry, and EditorState
steps.description: Implement KeyEvent (code, modifiers), CommandRegistry (register/lookup/execute named commands), and EditorState (aggregating buffer, cursor, viewport, commands, running flag, message). CommandRegistry maps String -> CommandHandler where CommandHandler is Native(fn). EditorState is the top-level state passed through the event loop. Include the Mode, Keymap stubs (just the type definitions, full implementation in M6).
acceptance_criteria: KeyEvent type represents single key inputs: character, arrow key, or Ctrl-modifier combination
CommandRegistry stores commands by name and can look them up by string
Command execution mutates EditorState (e.g., moves cursor, modifies message)
EditorState is the single mutable container: buffer, cursor, viewport, command registry, mode, running flag
EditorState initialization sets cursor to (0, 0), viewport to fit terminal, running=true, registries empty

steps.title: Terminal renderer with ratatui
steps.description: Implement the terminal renderer in alfred-tui. Uses crossterm for raw mode and ratatui for rendering. Renderer draws: buffer content (visible lines based on viewport), cursor position, message line (bottom row). The renderer takes &EditorState and produces a frame. No hardcoded UI beyond the text area and message line (gutter and status bar are plugins in M4/M5).
acceptance_criteria: Renderer enters raw mode on start and restores on exit
Buffer content renders as visible lines within viewport bounds
Cursor appears at the correct position in the terminal
Message line shows content of state.message at bottom of screen
Empty buffer renders without panic

steps.title: Event loop with hardcoded keybindings
steps.description: Implement the main event loop in alfred-tui. Reads crossterm events, dispatches to registered commands (or hardcoded handlers for M1). Loop: read event -> handle key -> update state -> render. Hardcoded bindings for M1: arrow keys -> cursor movement, Ctrl-Q -> quit. These will be replaced by plugin-defined keybindings in M6. Wire everything through alfred-bin main().
acceptance_criteria: Arrow keys move cursor in the correct direction
Ctrl-Q sets running=false and exits the event loop cleanly
Terminal is restored to normal mode on exit (even on error)
Opening a file from CLI argument displays its content
Scrolling works when cursor moves past viewport boundaries
Buffer is read-only in M1: no text insertion or deletion available (modification operations are not yet implemented)
